core_rule: The strongest Brand3 reading is the most precise interpretation the evidence can support.

failure_modes.name: Invented Intentionality
failure_modes.definition: Assigning motive, strategy, audience intent, or business objective to the brand without direct evidence.
examples: The brand seeks cultural authority.
The company is targeting sophisticated enterprise buyers.
The surface is designed to make users feel safe.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Perceptual language invites causal explanation, so writers fill in the internal strategy that would make a surface pattern make sense.
observable_warning_signs: verbs like seeks, intends, aims, wants, designed to, built to
audience claims without source support
strategic conclusions attached to aesthetic signals
because clauses that explain internal motivation
mitigation_strategies: Replace intention with supported reading.
Name the evidence type.
Add that internal intent is not observable from outside when needed.
Require human review for unstated strategic intent.

confidence_rules.high: Only when intent is directly stated by the brand or source.
confidence_rules.medium: Repeated source language consistently frames intent but product behavior is not visible.
confidence_rules.low: Intent is inferred from aesthetics, tone, layout, or category alone.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The owned copy frames the product as a system for enterprise workflow, which may support a reading of operational ambition.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand is pursuing enterprise authority.

failure_modes.name: Unsupported Emotional Projection
failure_modes.definition: Claiming a felt emotional state without tying it to observable mechanisms.
examples: The brand feels reassuring.
The experience is calming and supportive.
The interface creates trust.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Emotional temperature is useful, but mood words are easy to write without evidence.
observable_warning_signs: calm, warm, reassuring, supportive, trustworthy, inviting, human, confident
no reference to color, pacing, hierarchy, density, typography, motion, illustration, or copy
emotional outcome phrased as user effect
mitigation_strategies: Tie emotional temperature to mechanisms.
Keep user impact conditional.
Avoid therapeutic, psychological, or trust claims unless evidence supports them.

confidence_rules.medium: Surface signals and copy tone align.
confidence_rules.low: Emotion comes from category expectation or brand reputation.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: Soft color, low-density hierarchy, and friendly illustration can support a low-to-medium confidence reading of calm accessibility.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand makes users feel supported.

failure_modes.name: Cinematic Inflation
failure_modes.definition: Overusing cinematic or motion language when motion is absent, copy-only, or not concept-bearing.
examples: The brand creates a cinematic journey.
The experience has filmic momentum.
The motion system expresses transformation.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Motion-oriented patterns such as Guided Movement, Concept-Bearing Motion, and Threshold Pacing can become decorative if applied to static surfaces.
observable_warning_signs: cinematic, immersive, filmic, kinetic, choreographed, journey
no captured motion, sequence, transition, or interaction evidence
production terms treated as perceptual effects
mitigation_strategies: Separate motion claim from motion observation.
Use copy states when motion is source-described but not captured.
Require capture, video, or sequential evidence before high confidence.

confidence_rules.high: Direct motion evidence exists and the concept linkage is clear.
confidence_rules.medium: Source-stated motion has some supporting still-sequence evidence.
confidence_rules.low: Motion is inferred from static aesthetics or production language alone.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The case copy names Datamosh transitions, but concept-bearing motion remains unverified until motion evidence is reviewed.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand uses cinematic motion to symbolize disruption.

failure_modes.name: False Sophistication Language
failure_modes.definition: Using prestige-coded adjectives as if they were analysis.
examples: The brand is sophisticated.
The visual identity is premium and polished.
The system is elegant, modern, and refined.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Baseline LLM prose often defaults to generic quality language when evidence is thin, and perceptual augmentation can replace old generic language with new pseudo-premium vocabulary.
observable_warning_signs: sophisticated, premium, elegant, refined, polished, elevated, world-class
adjectives not followed by observable mechanism
quality words standing where pattern behavior should be
mitigation_strategies: Replace quality labels with surface behavior.
Ban prestige adjectives unless quoted or directly evidenced.
Ask what changed on the surface.

confidence_rules.high: Rare; only if the term is a direct source quote and labeled as such.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The surface uses restrained spacing, product-scale imagery, and short declarative copy.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand feels premium and sophisticated.

failure_modes.name: Weak Evidence Amplification
failure_modes.definition: Making sparse, copy-only, obstructed, or single-source evidence sound more conclusive than it is.
examples: The interface is intuitive based on a source claim.
The system is scalable based on case-study copy.
The brand lacks differentiation based only on missing evidence.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: The narrative layer tries to be useful even when evidence is thin.
observable_warning_signs: single URL drives broad conclusion
copy claims become product behavior
absence becomes defect
obstruction or missing capture disappears from prose
mitigation_strategies: Name evidence limits inside the observation.
Convert conclusions into limitations or review questions.
Keep copy-based material at medium or low confidence.
Do not infer failure from missing evidence.

confidence_rules.medium: Copy-based claims stated clearly as copy.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The source states a seamless interface, but current evidence only supports a copy-level guidance claim.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The product offers a seamless interface.

failure_modes.name: Aesthetic Hallucination
failure_modes.definition: Inventing visual details that are not present in the evidence pool.
examples: Soft gradients and rounded modules when no capture exists.
Dense navigation and code panels when only homepage copy was reviewed.
Large serif typography copied from another case.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Static perceptual hints include surface signals from prior cases, which can contaminate target observations if treated as facts.
observable_warning_signs: visual details not found in screenshots, HTML, or source text
FLOC case-specific signals appear in unrelated brand analysis
pattern examples leak into target observations
mitigation_strategies: Treat adapter hints as lenses only, never target facts.
Require every visual detail to map to evidence.
Do not mention Charms, D4DATA, Grandvalira, or their motifs unless in the evidence pool.

confidence_rules.high: Directly visible or directly extracted signals.
confidence_rules.low: Visual details imported from registry examples are invalid or require rewrite.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: If similar repeated forms appear in the target evidence, they may support a motif-control reading.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand uses soft light fields and ritual pacing when those signals come only from Charms.

failure_modes.name: Narrative Over-Binding
failure_modes.definition: Forcing multiple signals into a single coherent story when the evidence supports only partial or divergent readings.
examples: Every surface reinforces the same strategic narrative.
The ecosystem resolves complexity through a unified grammar.
All channels converge around trust and speed.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Reports prefer coherent stories, and perceptual patterns can make fragmented evidence feel artificially integrated.
observable_warning_signs: all, every, fully, resolves, unified, converges
no acknowledgement of divergent channels
contradiction smoothed into coherence
pattern names used to close uncertainty
mitigation_strategies: Preserve partiality.
Name channel divergence when present.
Use one available reading instead of the story.
Keep contradictions unresolved when evidence is unresolved.

confidence_rules.high: Multiple independent anchors support the same reading.
confidence_rules.medium: Two or more signals converge but source diversity is limited.
confidence_rules.low: The story is assembled from scattered or weak signals.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: Several owned-surface signals point toward operational clarity, while external corroboration remains limited.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand coherently expresses operational clarity across the ecosystem.

failure_modes.name: Perception Without Observable Grounding
failure_modes.definition: Applying perceptual pattern language without naming the signal that supports it.
examples: This is category-to-surface translation.
The brand uses guided movement.
The emotional temperature is controlled.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Registry vocabulary can become shorthand. Shorthand is unsafe in report prose unless anchored.
observable_warning_signs: pattern label appears without evidence
emotional temperature appears before observation
no quote, source, surface, channel, visual signal, or structural mechanism
mitigation_strategies: Every pattern sentence must include at least one surface mechanism.
Pattern labels should follow observations, not replace them.
If no mechanism can be named, do not use the pattern.

confidence_rules.high: Signal, cluster, and pattern are all present.
confidence_rules.medium: Signal exists but cluster is partial.
confidence_rules.low: Pattern is plausible but only one weak signal exists.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: Navigation density, code-oriented content, and implementation pathways support a category-to-surface reading.

failure_modes.name: Tension Fabrication
failure_modes.definition: Inventing a contradiction or tradeoff because the report expects one.
examples: The brand balances accessibility and authority with no evidence for either side.
There is a tension between calm and conversion without conversion evidence.
Minimalism conflicts with density when only one surface was reviewed.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Tension improves narrative quality, but not every evidence pool contains a real tension.
observable_warning_signs: between X and Y where X or Y has no evidence
tension language appears in every dimension
tension is generic enough to fit any brand
no contradiction, absence, mismatch, or tradeoff is named
mitigation_strategies: Require evidence for both sides.
Use no stable tension identified when needed.
Treat claim/evidence gaps as unverified, not automatically defects.

confidence_rules.medium: One side is direct and one side is copy-based.
confidence_rules.low: One side is inferred or absent.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The source claims modularity, but available evidence supports repeated motif control more clearly than component behavior.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand balances modularity and emotion without evidence for both.

failure_modes.name: Generic Premium Projection
failure_modes.definition: Reading premium status into restraint, minimalism, whitespace, or monochrome surfaces without evidence.
examples: The restrained layout signals premium positioning.
Minimalism makes the product feel luxury.
Whitespace creates a high-end perception.
failure_modes.why_it_happens: Premium is often used as a shortcut for restraint.
observable_warning_signs: premium, luxury, high-end, elevated attached to whitespace or restraint
market tier inferred from aesthetics
no pricing, audience, product, or third-party evidence
mitigation_strategies: Replace premium with observable restraint.
Require separate market-position evidence.
Treat premium as interpretation, never direct visual fact.

confidence_rules.high: Premium positioning is explicitly stated or externally corroborated.
confidence_rules.medium: Surface restraint aligns with product or category evidence.
confidence_rules.low: Premium is inferred from visual restraint alone.

failure_modes.acceptable_form: The surface is restrained: sparse copy, high whitespace, and controlled product imagery.
failure_modes.unacceptable_form: The brand reads as premium because it is restrained.

safe_perceptual_augmentation_zones: Direct screenshots, source quotes, HTML structure, or repeated visual signals are present.
The finding names a concrete surface mechanism.
The prose separates claim, observation, implication, and limitation.
Contradiction is recorded as claim/signal, not defect.
Emotional temperature is tied to pacing, density, hierarchy, color, motion, or interaction.
Weak evidence is written as a limitation or review question.
high_risk_narrative_zones: Famous-brand reputation likely fills evidence gaps.
Emotionally loaded categories such as wellness, finance, AI, and luxury.
Authority, trust, support, sophistication, leadership, or premium status claims.
Expected tension with no evidence-backed tradeoff.
Motion, cinematic, or interface claims stated in copy but not captured.

dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.risk: Invented audience readings, trust claims, authority claims, and emotional projection.
dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.control: Require source type and corroboration status.

dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.risk: False uniqueness, leadership language, and unsupported category distinction.
dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.control: Require comparison anchor or state that comparison is unavailable.

dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.risk: Cinematic inflation, momentum claims, and unsupported community or cultural energy.
dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.control: Require timestamped activity, motion evidence, or channel behavior.

dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.risk: Weak evidence amplification and treating thin public presence as a strategy verdict.
dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.control: Treat absence as evidence limitation unless the absence itself is measured and relevant.

dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.risk: Narrative over-binding and generic cohesion language.
dimensions_most_vulnerable_to_overreach.control: Separate repeated motif, design-system claim, and verified component behavior.

editorial_acceptance_gate: Does it name at least one observable signal?
Does it distinguish source claim from observed behavior?
Does every interpretation use conditional language unless directly stated?
Does it avoid prestige-coded adjectives as analysis?
Does it avoid importing signals from prior cases?
Does any tension have evidence on both sides?
Does emotional temperature cite the mechanism that creates it?
Does low-confidence material remain a limitation or review question?
Would the sentence still be true if brand fame were ignored?
Does the prose improve specificity without increasing invented intent?
